perturbation.original_text: Buyer represents that Buyer has sufficient financial resources to complete the transaction for the asking price and terms set forth herein. Buyer agrees to provide, upon request by Broker or Seller, financial statements, credit references and other pertinent information evidencing such financial sufficiency.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer represents that Buyer has sufficient financial resources to complete the transaction for the asking price and terms set forth herein. Buyer is not obligated to provide, unless a purchase agreement is executed, financial statements, credit references and other pertinent information evidencing such financial sufficiency.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by stating in Section 8 that the Buyer does not have to provide financial information 'unless a purchase agreement is executed' while the original text in the same section requires Buyer to furnish financial information 'upon request by Broker or Seller'. This change creates uncertainty about when the Buyer is obligated to provide financial documentation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer represents that Buyer has sufficient financial resources to complete the transaction for the asking price and terms set forth herein. Buyer is not obligated to provide, unless a purchase agreement is executed, financial statements, credit references and other pertinent information evidencing such financial sufficiency.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. Originally, the Buyer was obligated to provide financial information upon request. The modified text states that the Buyer is *not* obligated to provide this information unless a purchase agreement is executed. Since the Contradicted Text is the Changed Text, there's a direct inconsistency within that section regarding when the Buyer must provide financial information, resulting in a strong in-text contradiction, as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, where conflicting obligations are introduced.

perturbation.original_text: Buyer will not contact the Seller or Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents other than Broker for any reason whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Broker. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties will be made through or by Broker unless otherwise agreed to by Broker, in writing. Seller’s employees may not be aware of the sale of the Business and Buyer agrees not to disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, nor identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: Buyer will not contact the Seller or Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents other than Broker for any reason whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Broker. Buyer is authorized to contact the Seller to obtain clarification of materials after written notice to the Broker. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties will be made through or by Broker unless otherwise agreed to by Broker, in writing. Seller’s employees may not be aware of the sale of the Business and Buyer agrees not to disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, nor identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction. Section 3 initially states that the Buyer cannot contact the Seller or Seller's agents for any reason without written permission. The added sentence contradicts this by stating the 'Buyer is authorized to contact the Seller to obtain clarification of materials after written notice to the Broker' leading to uncertainty about the conditions under which the Buyer can contact the Seller.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Buyer will not contact the Seller or Seller's employees, customers, suppliers or agents other than Broker for any reason whatsoever without the prior written consent of the Broker. Buyer is authorized to contact the Seller to obtain clarification of materials after written notice to the Broker. All contacts with the Seller or such other parties will be made through or by Broker unless otherwise agreed to by Broker, in writing. Seller’s employees may not be aware of the sale of the Business and Buyer agrees not to disclose to Seller’s employees that the Business is for sale, nor identify that Buyer is a possible purchaser of the Business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text prohibited any contact without prior written consent, creating a blanket restriction. The changed text creates an exception, allowing contact for clarification after notice, directly conflicting with the original blanket restriction. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, further demonstrating the direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The performance and construction of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania. All sums due hereunder shall be payable at the office of the Broker in Bucks County, Pennsylvania. The parties agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for Bucks County, Pennsylvania to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Claims for injunctive relief may be filed in any court with appropriate jurisdiction over the matter. All other claims must be arbitrated in accordance with section 11 below.
perturbation.changed_text: The performance and construction of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, except for disputes regarding intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Delaware. All sums due hereunder shall be payable at the office of the Broker in Bucks County, Pennsylvania. The parties agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for Bucks County, Pennsylvania to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Claims for injunctive relief may be filed in any court with appropriate jurisdiction over the matter. All other claims must be arbitrated in accordance with section 11 below.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 9 by stating that the agreement is governed by Pennsylvania law 'except for disputes regarding intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Delaware.' The initial statement declares Pennsylvania law governs 'The performance and construction of this Agreement,' but the added clause creates ambiguity about which state's laws apply, thus contradicting the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The performance and construction of this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, except for disputes regarding intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Delaware. All sums due hereunder shall be payable at the office of the Broker in Bucks County, Pennsylvania. The parties agree to the exclusive jurisdiction of the state and federal courts in and for Bucks County, Pennsylvania to enforce the terms of this Agreement. Claims for injunctive relief may be filed in any court with appropriate jurisdiction over the matter. All other claims must be arbitrated in accordance with section 11 below.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 9 to state that the agreement is governed by Pennsylvania law 'except for disputes regarding intellectual property, which shall be governed by the laws of Delaware.' This change introduces a contradiction within the agreement itself, because the contradicted text is Section 9, which states that Pennsylvania law governs 'The performance and construction of this Agreement.' This creates an inconsistency as to which state's laws apply, contradicting the initial statement and fulfilling the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the Buyer, Buyer's heirs, executors, successors, assigns, administrators or representatives. If any provision of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remainder of the provisions hereof shall remain in full force and effect and this Agreement shall be construed as if such invalid, void or unenforceable provision had not been contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the Buyer, Buyer's heirs, executors, successors, assigns, administrators or representatives, except that the Buyer's obligations regarding confidentiality shall not extend to heirs or assigns. If any provision of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remainder of the provisions hereof shall remain in full force and effect and this Agreement shall be construed as if such invalid, void or unenforceable provision had not been contained herein.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction in Section 10. The first part of the section indicates 'This Agreement shall be binding upon the Buyer, Buyer's heirs, executors, successors, assigns, administrators or representatives,' but this is directly contradicted by adding 'except that the Buyer's obligations regarding confidentiality shall not extend to heirs or assigns.' This modification creates uncertainty about whether confidentiality obligations are binding on the Buyer's heirs or assigns, making the section inconsistent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon the Buyer, Buyer's heirs, executors, successors, assigns, administrators or representatives, except that the Buyer's obligations regarding confidentiality shall not extend to heirs or assigns. If any provision of this Agreement shall be held to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remainder of the provisions hereof shall remain in full force and effect and this Agreement shall be construed as if such invalid, void or unenforceable provision had not been contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the general rule that the agreement is binding upon the Buyer's heirs, executors, successors, assigns, administrators or representatives. Specifically, it states that the Buyer's obligations regarding confidentiality shall not extend to heirs or assigns. Since the contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text, this means that the first part of the section creates a binding agreement on heirs and assigns, but the second part creates an exception for confidentiality. This modification creates a direct conflict within the text about whether the confidentiality obligations are binding on the Buyer's heirs or assigns.

perturbation.original_text: Any controversy between the parties arising out of the terms, obligations, covenants or conditions of this Agreement, shall be arbitrated in Bucks County, Pennsylvania on written request of one (1) party served on the other. Such arbitration shall be under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator shall have no authority to change any provisions of this agreement; the arbitrator’s sole authority shall be to interpret or apply the provisions of this Agreement. The expenses of arbitration conducted pursuant to this paragraph shall be born by the parties in such proportion as the Arbitrator shall decide.
perturbation.changed_text: Any controversy between the parties arising out of the terms, obligations, covenants or conditions of this Agreement, with the exception of disputes regarding the interpretation of financial statements provided by the buyer, shall be arbitrated in Bucks County, Pennsylvania on written request of one (1) party served on the other. Such arbitration shall be under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator shall have no authority to change any provisions of this agreement; the arbitrator’s sole authority shall be to interpret or apply the provisions of this Agreement. The expenses of arbitration conducted pursuant to this paragraph shall be born by the parties in such proportion as the Arbitrator shall decide.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 11. Initially, the section states that 'Any controversy between the parties arising out of the terms, obligations, covenants or conditions of this Agreement, shall be arbitrated'. However, the changed text adds 'with the exception of disputes regarding the interpretation of financial statements provided by the buyer', thus creating ambiguity about whether disputes regarding the interpretation of financial statements fall under the arbitration clause or are to be resolved elsewhere.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any controversy between the parties arising out of the terms, obligations, covenants or conditions of this Agreement, with the exception of disputes regarding the interpretation of financial statements provided by the buyer, shall be arbitrated in Bucks County, Pennsylvania on written request of one (1) party served on the other. Such arbitration shall be under the rules of the American Arbitration Association. The arbitrator shall have no authority to change any provisions of this agreement; the arbitrator’s sole authority shall be to interpret or apply the provisions of this Agreement. The expenses of arbitration conducted pursuant to this paragraph shall be born by the parties in such proportion as the Arbitrator shall decide.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an exception ('with the exception of disputes regarding the interpretation of financial statements provided by the buyer') to the original blanket statement that 'Any controversy between the parties arising out of the terms, obligations, covenants or conditions of this Agreement, shall be arbitrated'. The contradicted text contains the exact same change. This creates a potential contradiction within Section 11 regarding whether disputes over financial statements are subject to arbitration, thus creating ambiguity about whether disputes regarding the interpretation of financial statements fall under the arbitration clause or are to be resolved elsewhere. Thus there is an inconsistency leading to a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The provisions hereof cannot be modified, amended, supplemented or rescinded without the written consent of Broker and this Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding of the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: The provisions hereof can be modified, amended, supplemented or rescinded by email communication between the buyer and seller; the broker's consent is not required. This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding of the parties hereto.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction. Section 13 initially requires 'written consent of Broker' for any modifications. The added phrase states that modifications 'can be modified, amended, supplemented or rescinded by email communication between the buyer and seller; the broker's consent is not required.' This directly contradicts the earlier requirement and creates uncertainty as to whether modifications are valid without the broker's consent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The provisions hereof can be modified, amended, supplemented or rescinded by email communication between the buyer and seller; the broker's consent is not required. This Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding of the parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text by stating that modifications can be made via email between the buyer and seller without the broker's consent, while the original text requires the written consent of the broker for any modifications. This creates a strong in-text contradiction regarding the procedure for modifying the agreement, making it unclear whether the broker's consent is necessary.
